etymology.description: originally written as 又(又) (yòu, "again") -> bronze inscriptions added 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") as a distinguishing mark joining phonetic 又 to form 右
etymology.interpretation: 又(又) (yòu, "again") provided the phonetic value, initially serving as a phonetic loan for 'right'. The character 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") was added later without semantic significance to differentiate it from other uses of 又, finalizing the character 右 for the direction "right".

examples.chinese: 他的右手抬起来了(他的右手抬起來了)。
小猫藏在沙发的右侧(小貓藏在沙發的右側)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo māo cáng zài shāfā de yòu cè.
examples.english: The kitten is hiding on the right side of the sofa.
